steps.title: Use the initial value to pin down the coefficient
steps.content: An exponential function $h(x) = a \cdot b^x$ gives $h(0) = a$. The table shows $h(0) = 1.25$, so $a = 1.25$. This already rules out the choices with coefficient $1.20$.

steps.title: Solve for the base using another row of the table
Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $1.20(0.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value and the base $0.25 < 1$ would shrink the heights instead of growing them. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $1.20(1.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value $1.20$ at $x = 0$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $1.25(0.20)^x$ starts at the right value but the base $0.20$ would make heights collapse toward zero. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $1.25(1.20)^x$ gives $h(0) = 1.25$ and $h(2) = 1.25(1.44) = 1.80$, matching the table. Matches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
